w:r.w:t: Revenue Assurance | DSM & Network | Customer Experience | Field Operations
Prepared by: Revenue Assurance & CX Strategy
Applicable to: Collections, DSM, Field Ops, Customer Care, Network Planning, Billing Ops

The Consumer Persona & Scenario Intelligence Framework is a unified analytical layer that converts raw operational data — from smart meters, billing systems, payment records, CRM, and consumer apps — into actionable consumer-level intelligence.

Every consumer receives six scores monthly. These scores are combined into personas. Personas drive targeted, differentiated actions across six operational domains: collections, DSM, field operations, customer experience, network planning, and regulatory risk management.
Predict and prevent payment defaults before they occur — reducing field enforcement cost by 30–40%
Route defaulters correctly: digital recovery vs field visit based on engagement level
Target DSM and Demand Response programmes at consumers with genuine flexible load
Identify consumers whose DT/feeder stress can be resolved by behaviour change vs infrastructure upgrade
Pre-empt billing complaints and regulatory escalations before they leave the DISCOM
Personalise communications by channel, timing, and message based on digital readiness
The framework operates in four sequential layers:

Data is refreshed monthly, aligned to the billing cycle. Each consumer holds one analytical record per billing month, enabling month-on-month trend tracking and intervention effectiveness measurement.
Every consumer receives all six scores each billing month. Persona assignment and action routing are driven entirely by combinations of these scores. No score is used in isolation.
Payment delays, default probability, consistency score, arrears status
Peak hour usage, DT coincidence level, NILM appliance flags (AC / EV / pump)
Complaint frequency, TAT history, escalation flags, bill shock indicator, repeat flag
App logins, notification opens, digital payment flag, preferred channel, contact time
Flexible load score + NILM flags + TOD sensitivity + peak coincidence level
Complaint unresolved >30 days, escalation flag set, complaint risk >70 AND TAT >15 days
N – No action | Y – Senior officer escalation

The Regulatory Risk Flag is a binary indicator (Y/N) set on a consumer's monthly record when there is a credible risk that their complaint or grievance will escalate beyond the DISCOM to an external regulatory or legal body.
An existing complaint has not been resolved within 30 days — the consumer now has the legal right to approach the Ombudsman or State Consumer Forum
The CRM escalation flag is already set, meaning the consumer has explicitly threatened or initiated external escalation
Complaint Risk Score is above 70 AND average resolution TAT exceeds 15 days — a predictive trigger before escalation occurs
Complaint is in billing or metering category AND no revised bill has been issued within the investigation window
DISCOM internal helpline → DISCOM Ombudsman → State Consumer Forum (Consumer Protection Act) → SERC (tariff / supply grievances)
Why it is different from Complaint Risk Score:

Complaint Risk Score measures the probability of any complaint being raised. The Regulatory Risk Flag is narrower — it measures the probability of that complaint leaving the DISCOM's control and entering a regulatory or legal forum. A consumer can have a high complaint risk score but a clear regulatory flag if complaints are being resolved quickly. Complaint Risk Score = volume risk. Regulatory Risk Flag = institutional / legal risk.

Each consumer is assigned a primary persona and, where applicable, a secondary persona each billing month. Primary persona = the dominant score signal. Secondary persona = the next-highest signal where it crosses a meaningful threshold.
Six personas covering the full spectrum from reliable payers to write-off risk. These drive collections routing, enforcement timing, and channel selection.
Pays within 3 days of bill receipt. Never defaults. High payment consistency score. Responds proactively to communications.
Auto-debit nudge + loyalty offer. No enforcement action required.
Pays 8–18 days after due date. Forgets due dates due to busy schedule. Not wilful. Responds immediately to reminders. The 'Priya' archetype.
WhatsApp D-3 pre-due reminder with UPI deep link. Highest ROI intervention.
Seasonal delays linked to festival season, rent, school fees, or salary cycle. Pays eventually. Needs flexibility, not enforcement.
60-day instalment plan via WhatsApp. Flexible due date opt-in where regulatory limits allow.
Pays only after field visit or formal notice. Habitual pattern across 4+ cycles per year. Low engagement. Field-dependent.
Pre-payment meter installation + security deposit review. Field visit with advance notice.
Payment deliberately withheld due to billing dispute, meter error, or slab-crossing confusion. Active complaint on file. Not a willful defaulter.
Rev Risk: 55–80 Complaint Risk: 65+ Bill Shock: Y
Freeze enforcement. Resolve billing dispute first. Reissue bill. Collect only after resolution.
Tenant has vacated. Landlord unreachable. Bills accruing with no payment or engagement. Meter access unavailable.
Disconnect supply. Issue legal notice. Adjust security deposit against arrears.

Six personas identifying consumers by their impact on network infrastructure and their potential for Demand Side Management participation. These personas drive DSM programme targeting, network investment deferral, and peak load management.
Peak usage aligns precisely with DT/feeder peak. Contributes directly to overload risk. NILM flags heavy AC usage. Non-flexible load pattern.
Peak Impact: 75+ Flex Load: <35 Coincidence: High
DR enrolment offer with financial incentive. TOD tariff information campaign.
High flexible load score. Usage pattern can be shifted off-peak. Willing to participate in Demand Response if incentivised. Ideal DSM target.
Flex Load Score: 65+ Peak Impact: 40–75 Engagement: 45+
DR programme enrolment + incentive offer via app notification. Priority DSM outreach.
NILM algorithm has detected EV charging signature. Daytime charging is increasing peak coincidence. Candidate for night tariff shift.
NILM EV: Y Peak Impact: 55+ Engagement: 50+
EV night tariff offer + smart charging schedule guidance. Long-term: EV tariff enrolment.
High renewable score based on consumption profile, location, and property type. High monthly consumption makes rooftop solar economically viable.
Rooftop solar subsidy scheme offer. Net metering application guidance. State scheme eligibility.
Consistently drawing more kW than sanctioned load. Load violation flag active multiple months. Risk of equipment damage and DT overload.
Load Violation: Y (3+ months) Peak Impact: 60+
Load enhancement notice + field survey for formal load revision. Revenue leakage correction.
Below-average consumption relative to household profile. Low peak contribution. Possible BEE star-rated appliance use. Good network citizen.
Efficiency certification acknowledgement. Loyalty reward. No DSM action required.
Three personas capturing complaint behaviour and regulatory risk exposure. These personas drive customer care prioritisation, resolution SLA allocation, and legal risk management.
Three or more complaints in a quarter. Same underlying issue recurring across cycles. Escalation flag active. High frustration level.
Complaint Risk: 75+ Escalation Flag: Y Repeat Flag: Y
Dedicated case manager assigned. Root cause investigation. Compensation consideration.
Complaint has already been or is about to be escalated to SERC, Ombudsman, or State Consumer Forum. Maximum regulatory exposure. Penalty risk.
Reg Risk Flag: Y Complaint Risk: 80+ TAT: >30 days
Senior officer direct outreach. Fast-track resolution. Formal written response. Legal team loop-in.
Frequent bill shock flags. Slab-crossing confusion. Does not understand tariff structure. Likely to dispute or withhold payment without proactive explanation.
Bill Shock Flag: Y Bill Variance: >30% Complaint Risk: 45–70
Proactive plain-language slab explanation via WhatsApp before due date. Prevent dispute from forming.
Three personas based on digital channel maturity. These determine which communication channel, message format, and timing will be used for all outreach — collections, DSM, billing, and CX.
Daily app user. Opens notifications within minutes. UPI-first payer. Self-service capable. Responds before enforcement is needed.
Engagement: 75+ Digital Payment: Y App Logins: 15+/mo
App-first all communications. Auto-debit setup nudge. Self-service escalation path. No field contact.
Responds reliably to WhatsApp messages. Ignores app push notifications. Medium digital readiness. Prefers conversational interface over app.
WhatsApp-led all journeys. Embed UPI deep link directly in message. Migrate to app over time.
Cash payment at counter or CSC. Does not use app. IVR or field agent is the primary contact channel. Low digital readiness score.
Engagement: <25 Digital Payment: N App Logins: 0
IVR automated reminder + CSC payment guidance. Field agent visit for recovery. Physical notice delivery.
Three composite personas combining multiple dimensions. These represent consumers where a single-dimension view is insufficient and a coordinated multi-team response is required.
Low revenue risk + high monthly bill value + high engagement. The most commercially valuable and easiest-to-serve segment. Protect relationship.
Rev Risk: <20 Bill Value: High bucket Engagement: 80+
Premium CX channel. Proactive consumption insights. First access to new tariff and scheme offers.
High monthly bill + high revenue risk = maximum revenue exposure per consumer. Warrants dedicated senior collections resource.
Rev Risk: 65+ Bill Value: High bucket Overdue: >15 days
Senior collections agent. Part-payment offer. Instalment plan. Weekly follow-up until resolved.
Active default AND active complaint simultaneously. Billing dispute is the likely root cause of payment withholding. Requires coordinated CX + collections response.
Rev Risk: 60+ Complaint Risk: 65+ Bill Shock: Y
Collections team pauses enforcement. CX team resolves complaint. Joint handoff after resolution.

Scenarios are the operational heart of the framework. Each scenario defines the exact score combination that triggers it, the recommended action, and the team responsible. These are implemented as rule-based triggers in the analytical engine.
Revenue Risk 25–55 Engagement > 40 No active complaint Due date within 3 days
WhatsApp reminder D-3 before due date. Include exact bill amount + UPI deep link. No human intervention required. Estimated impact: 20–25% reduction in accidental defaults.
Revenue Risk > 55 Overdue > 15 days Digital path: Engagement > 50 Field path: Engagement < 30
Digital path: WhatsApp + IVR outbound call. Field path: Scheduled field agent visit with 30-minute advance WhatsApp notice to consumer. Saves 40–50% of field visit cost.
Revenue Risk > 65 Bill Value = High bucket (> Rs. 5,000) Overdue > 15 days
Senior collections agent assigned. Outbound call with consumer name + amount. Part-payment acceptance to break all-or-nothing barrier. Structured instalment plan.
Revenue Risk > 50 Complaint Risk > 60 Bill Shock Flag = Y Active complaint open
Enforce freeze: no field visit, no disconnection notice while complaint is open. Billing team investigates within 7 days. Revised bill issued. Collections resumes only after resolution.
Revenue Risk 45–70 Overdue 15–30 days No prior instalment plan First or second default this year
Offer 2–3 month instalment plan entirely via WhatsApp. Consumer responds '1' to accept. UPI mandate set up in-flow. No DISCOM office visit required. Minimum 50% upfront.
Revenue Risk > 70 Field visits > 3 in last 6 months Consistent chronic pattern
Pre-payment meter installation. Security deposit top-up review. Consumer briefed on credit loading process. Eliminates repeat field visit cycle permanently.
DT peak loading > 90% Consumer Peak Impact > 70 Consumer Flex Load > 55 Engagement > 45
DR enrolment offer with incentive via app notification. Target flexible consumers on overloaded DTs first. Avoids premature DT upgrade. Estimated capex deferral: Rs. 15–40L per DT.
NILM EV Flag = Y Peak coincidence = High Engagement > 50 Daytime charging detected
EV night tariff offer + smart charging schedule personalised to consumer's usage pattern. Shift charging from 6–10pm to 11pm–6am. Reduces peak coincidence by 60–80% for this consumer.
DT loading > 85% DSM path: >40% of DT consumers have Flex Score > 60 Upgrade path: Infra age > 15 yrs + all loads non-flexible
DSM-first protocol: run DR programme for 3 months. If peak not reduced by 15%+, trigger capex upgrade review. Prevents premature network investment.
Renewable Score > 60 Monthly consumption > 250 kWh Engagement > 45 Residential / own property
Rooftop solar subsidy scheme offer via app. Net metering application guide. State scheme eligibility check. Reduces consumer's long-term consumption and DT load.
Load Violation Flag = Y (3+ consecutive months) Actual load > 130% of sanctioned load
Field survey to formally reassess sanctioned load. Issue load enhancement notice. Recover additional charges retrospectively. Correct DT loading calculations.
Peak Coincidence = High TOD Sensitivity = Medium-High Smart meter installed Engagement > 50
Educate consumer on TOD tariff savings potential via personalised WhatsApp message showing estimated savings. Facilitate tariff change application.
Complaint Risk > 75 Escalation Flag = Y Resolution TAT > 15 days Complaint open > 20 days
Senior officer direct outreach to consumer within 24 hours. Fast-track resolution SLA. Compensation consideration. Formal written response. Legal team loop-in.
Bill Shock Flag = Y Bill variance vs 6-month avg > 30% New bill generated today
Proactive WhatsApp message before consumer calls. Plain-language slab explanation: 'You used 230 units. First 100 units at Rs. 3.50, next 130 at Rs. 5.50.' Reduces dispute calls by ~35%.
Complaint Risk > 65 Repeat Complaint Flag = Y Bill Shock Flag = Y Consumption variance > 40%
Meter accuracy test initiated. Full billing audit. Senior billing officer proactive outreach. Likely meter fault or tariff application error. Do not send collections notice until resolved.
Estimated Bills >= 2 consecutive cycles Complaint Risk > 40
Prioritise meter reading appointment scheduling. Flag for smart meter installation if not already assigned. Estimated billing is a leading predictor of disputes and defaults.
Revenue Risk > 70 Engagement < 25 Overdue > 20 days All digital channels exhausted
Cluster field visits by DT/area to minimise travel cost. Agent sends WhatsApp 30 minutes before arrival. Script includes name + amount + resolution options. Target same-visit payment.
Field visits > 3 in last 6 months Revenue Risk consistently > 65 Same consumer each cycle
Root cause analysis: is this a willful defaulter or a structural access issue? If willful: pre-pay meter. If access issue (tenant/landlord dynamic): registered letter + landlord contact.
Overdue = Day 28–29 Revenue Risk > 70 Engagement: Any level
Final WhatsApp + SMS with exact amount and payment link. Note that disconnection will occur tomorrow if not paid. For medium-engagement consumers: this is highest-conversion moment.
Payment Method: UPI regular user Auto-debit: Not set up Default pattern: 1–3 times/year
Guided auto-debit setup flow within utility app or BBPS. Include monthly cap consent (consumer sets maximum amount). Eliminates accidental defaults entirely for enrolled consumers.
Payment: Cash/counter Engagement Score: 20–40 Smartphone detected in CRM
Field agent or CSC staff guides consumer to make first digital payment on-site. Follow up with WhatsApp setup. Progressive digital adoption pathway.
Engagement Score: Any Best Contact Time: Available from app data
Schedule all reminder and outreach messages at consumer's known best contact time window. Increases open rate by 25–35%. Reduces 'wrong time' complaint calls.
Complaint Risk > 65 Bill Shock Flag = Y Consumption spike: current month > 150% of 6-month avg Repeat Complaint: Y
Do not send collections notice. Initiate meter test + billing audit. Assign senior billing officer. Resolve root cause before any revenue recovery action. Prevent regulatory escalation.
NILM EV: Y Peak Impact > 75 Flex Load > 60 Engagement > 55
Highest-value DSM opportunity. EV consumer causing peak stress but with flexibility to shift. Offer night tariff + smart charging + DR enrolment simultaneously. Personalised savings projection.
The matrices below show how persona assignment and action routing work when two dimensions are read together. These are the core logic tables used by the analytical engine.
This matrix drives the primary collections routing decision: digital, IVR, or field.
This matrix drives the CX and collections coordination decision. The key insight: high complaint risk always takes precedence over collections in the action sequence.
This matrix drives the DSM vs network investment decision. The bottom-right cell (high peak + low flexibility) is the infrastructure upgrade trigger.
Persona assignment is rule-based and explainable. Rules are evaluated in priority order. The first matching rule sets the primary persona. The second-highest signal sets the secondary persona.
Collections routing: digital or field based on engagement
Revenue Risk > 65 AND Bill Value = High
Complaint Risk > 70 AND Escalation Flag = Y
CX resolves first. Collections resumes after.
Immediate DR enrolment offer with incentive.
Flag DT/feeder for network planning review.
EV night tariff offer + smart charging.
Bill Shock Flag = Y AND Complaint Risk > 45
Proactive slab explanation before due date.
The following KPIs measure framework effectiveness across all operational domains. Baseline measurement should be taken at framework launch; targets are reviewed quarterly.
% who pay within 48 hrs of Day 15 overdue notice
% of field visits resulting in same-visit payment or plan
Flexible load consumers enrolled in Demand Response
Validates DSM-first protocol before upgrade approval
Days from ticket raise to revised bill issuance
Complaints that reach Ombudsman or SERC
Post-payment reconnection SLA — critical for satisfaction

The Consumer Persona & Scenario Intelligence Framework gives every team in the DISCOM a single, consistent view of each consumer — updated every billing month — with a clear recommended action attached.
The framework is explainable (every persona is traceable to specific score inputs), regulator-safe (no black-box AI), and scalable (monthly batch processing on existing data infrastructure).
Phase 1 — Data foundation: Implement the 6-table Consumer 360 data model. Validate attribute extraction from MDMS, billing, payment, CRM, and app systems.
Phase 2 — Scoring engine: Build the 6 scoring algorithms. Run shadow scoring for 2 billing cycles to validate distributions and thresholds.
Phase 3 — Persona assignment: Deploy rule-based persona assignment. Integrate with CRM and collections workflow for action routing.
Phase 4 — LLM explainability layer: Connect analytical outputs to LLM-based query interface for business teams to interrogate consumer, DT, feeder, and area-level insights in natural language.
Phase 5 — Feedback loop: Measure KPIs monthly. Refine thresholds based on actual intervention outcomes. Add secondary persona routing.
For questions on implementation, data model specifications, or scenario prioritisation, contact the Revenue Assurance & CX Strategy team.
End of document — Consumer Persona & Scenario Intelligence Framework | v1.0 | April 2026
